# UC-13.4.4 · Model Version Deployment Tracking

## Description

Knowing which model revision serves production supports rollback, audit, and safety reviews when behavior or cost shifts after a rollout.

## Value

Knowing which model revision serves production supports rollback, audit, and safety reviews when behavior or cost shifts after a rollout.

## Implementation

Log model id from inference gateway on each request; for self-hosted models, ingest deployment events with image tag or `MODEL_ID` env. Maintain a lookup of approved model versions per environment. Alert on requests referencing undeployed or deprecated model strings.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: CI/CD webhooks, Kubernetes labels, LLM gateway config audit.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=openai:api`, `sourcetype=k8s:deployment`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Log model id from inference gateway on each request; for self-hosted models, ingest deployment events with image tag or `MODEL_ID` env. Maintain a lookup of approved model versions per environment. Alert on requests referencing undeployed or deprecated model strings.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
(index=ai_ops sourcetype="openai:api") OR (index=platform sourcetype="k8s:deployment")
| eval model_id=coalesce(model, model_name, image_tag)
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen, latest(model_id) as current_model by deployment, namespace, environment
| sort environment, deployment
```

Understanding this SPL

**Model Version Deployment Tracking** — Knowing which model revision serves production supports rollback, audit, and safety reviews when behavior or cost shifts after a rollout.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=openai:api`, `sourcetype=k8s:deployment`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): CI/CD webhooks, Kubernetes labels, LLM gateway config audit. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ai_ops, platform; **sourcetype**: openai:api, k8s:deployment. Those sourcetypes align with what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ai_ops, index=platform, sourcetype="openai:api". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **model_id** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by deployment, namespace, environment** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm counts match the source or gateway for the same minutes; verify deduplication and routing explain any gap.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (environment × model version), Timeline (version changes), Single value (unapproved model calls).

## SPL

```spl
(index=ai_ops sourcetype="openai:api") OR (index=platform sourcetype="k8s:deployment")
| eval model_id=coalesce(model, model_name, image_tag)
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen, latest(model_id) as current_model by deployment, namespace, environment
| sort environment, deployment
```

## Visualization

Table (environment × model version), Timeline (version changes), Single value (unapproved model calls).
